modules.name: Auto Dialer
modules.description: Outbound calling automation module. Contains campaign management with multiple dialing modes (Preview, Progressive, Predictive, Agentless), record list management, and DNC compliance tools. Accessible via top navigation 'Auto dialer' tab.

features.name: Campaign Management
features.description: Create, manage and monitor outbound calling campaigns through a 4-step wizard. Campaigns can be in states: Preparing, Scheduled, Running, Ready, Completed, Paused, Incomplete.
user_steps: Navigate to Auto Dialer > Campaigns
Click 'Create campaign' button
Step 1: Fill in General Settings (name, description, timezone, dates, business hours, phone numbers, local presence)
Step 2: Configure Dialer Settings (choose dialer mode and mode-specific parameters)
Step 3: Add agents to Agent List
Step 4: Upload or select a Record List
Save and launch campaign

campaign_statuses.Preparing: Business number, agent, or record list hasn't been assigned yet
campaign_statuses.Scheduled: Campaign is set to start at a future date

campaign_edit_rules: Running and Paused campaigns allow limited edits only (general settings only, limited dialer settings for Progressive and Predictive modes)
To edit: click three-dot menu then Edit
To delete: click three-dot menu then Delete
features.business_value: Enables teams to run structured outbound calling campaigns with full control over scheduling, staffing, and contact lists.

features.name: Preview Dialing Mode
features.description: Agent is provided with a preview of the contact's information before initiating the call. The agent decides when to place the call. Best for small teams with manual review.
user_steps: In Dialer Settings (Step 2), select 'Preview' mode tab
Configure Preview time (how long agent has to review contact info)
Configure Wrap-up time (post-call wrap time)
Configure Max ring time (maximum ring duration for contact)
Set Dialing Attempts: Default retry period, Max attempts per record
Configure System Disposition actions (Retry/Final per outcome)
Configure Agent Disposition options (which outcomes agents can select)
features.business_value: Gives agents context before calling, improving conversation quality for high-value or complex contacts. Best for small teams requiring manual review.

features.name: Progressive Dialing Mode
features.description: System dials one call per available agent, maintaining steady flow. Best for small to medium teams.
user_steps: Select 'Progressive' mode tab in Dialer Settings
features.business_value: Balances automation with control — no abandoned calls, steady agent workload.

features.name: Predictive Dialing Mode
features.description: Algorithm-driven mass calling for large teams. Dials multiple contacts simultaneously to maximize call efficiency and reduce agent idle time.
user_steps: Select 'Predictive' mode tab in Dialer Settings
features.business_value: Maximizes agent productivity for large outbound teams by minimizing idle time between calls.

features.name: Agentless Dialing Mode
features.description: Automated outbound calling without live agents. Suitable for IVR-based campaigns, surveys, or broadcast messages.
user_steps: Select 'Agentless' mode tab in Dialer Settings
features.business_value: Enables fully automated outreach at scale without requiring agent staffing.

features.name: Local Presence
features.description: Selects the best business number based on the contact's area code. Makes calls appear local to the prospect, increasing answer rates. Uses only business numbers that match contact's location; some selected numbers may not be used. Calls are distributed evenly among matching numbers. If no match is found, Auto dialer uses all selected numbers which may hurt reputation and lower answer rates. Recommends purchasing additional matching numbers.
user_steps: In General Settings (Step 1), assign multiple business numbers
Toggle on 'Local Presence'
System automatically matches caller ID to contact area code at call time
features.business_value: Increases answer rates by displaying a local number to prospects instead of an unknown toll-free or out-of-state number.

features.name: Business Hours Scheduling
features.description: Configure per-day calling windows for a campaign. Each day (Sunday-Saturday) can be enabled/disabled with custom start and end times (AM/PM toggle), or set to Full Day. Monday through Friday are checked by default.
user_steps: In General Settings (Step 1), scroll to Business Hours section
Enable/disable each day with checkbox (Monday-Friday on by default)
Set start time (e.g. 08:00) and end time (e.g. 09:00) with AM/PM toggle per day
Or check 'Full day' for unrestricted daily calling
features.business_value: Ensures calls are placed only during legally and operationally acceptable hours, applied per the campaign timezone.

features.name: DNC Compliance (National DNC List Scrubber)
features.description: MightyCall is integrated with scrubbing services to verify dialing lists against 'Do Not Call' lists and reduce the risk of TCPA and DNC violations and lawsuits. When enabled, automatically removes registered numbers from campaign lists. Also supports a Local DNC List that can be managed separately.
user_steps: Toggle on/off National DNC list scrubber
When enabled, system automatically removes DNC-registered numbers from campaign lists
Manage Local DNC List: Add more, Replace, Delete all, or Export to CSV
features.business_value: Ensures compliance with TCPA and national DNC regulations, protecting the business from legal liability.

features.name: Local DNC List
features.description: Internal Do Not Call list managed within MightyCall. Agents can add numbers to the Local DNC list directly after a call via a checkbox in the call outcome panel. Can also be managed in bulk via Add more, Replace, Delete all, and Export to CSV actions.
user_steps: During a call, agent checks 'Add the number to Do not call list' checkbox in the call outcome panel
Or manage bulk entries via Local DNC List menu: Add more, Replace, Delete all, Export to CSV
features.business_value: Gives agents and managers control over internal DNC compliance, preventing repeat calls to contacts who opt out.

features.name: System Disposition
features.description: Automated handling rules for call outcomes not controlled by agents. Each outcome can be set to Retry (with configurable retry period and attempts per number) or Final.
features.business_value: Automates retry logic and eliminates manual queue management for unreachable contacts.

features.name: Agent Disposition
features.description: Configurable set of call outcome options that agents select after each call. Each disposition is marked as Final (removes contact from dial queue). Agents see these options in the 'SELECT CALL OUTCOME' panel after a call.
features.business_value: Standardizes agent call outcomes, drives retry logic, and feeds reporting/analytics.

features.name: Record List Management
features.description: Upload and manage contact lists (CSV format) for use in campaigns. Lists show record count, unique area codes, and coverage score. Files over 15 MB may take 5-7 minutes to upload. A template is available for download.
user_steps: In campaign creation Step 4 (Record List), upload a CSV file or drag-and-drop
Or select an existing record list from the Available lists panel
Download the template for correct CSV format
Review coverage score after list is attached
Use three-dot menu on uploaded list to View details
features.business_value: Enables targeted outreach by attaching specific contact databases to each campaign.

features.name: Coverage Score
features.description: A percentage metric showing how many unique contact area codes in the record list are covered by the campaign's assigned local and overlay numbers. Displayed per record list as a fraction and percentage. Warning shown when local presence may be less effective if some client area codes are not covered by selected numbers.
user_steps: Attach a record list to a campaign
Assign local business numbers in General Settings
Coverage Score auto-calculates and displays in the Record List step and Campaigns table
features.business_value: Helps managers optimize local presence coverage to maximize answer rates across different geographic regions.

features.name: Agent Workspace (Active Campaign)
features.description: The agent interface during an active preview dialer campaign. Shows wrap-up countdown timer, contact list with tabs (Unanswered, My recents, Clients, Team), conversation thread with messages/images/call recordings, and a call outcome selection panel.
user_steps: Agent receives preview of contact information
Agent decides when to initiate the call
During wrap-up, countdown timer displayed (e.g. 'Wrap-up 00:30')
After call, agent selects outcome from 'SELECT CALL OUTCOME' panel
Optionally check 'Add the number to Do not call list' to add to Internal DNC list
features.business_value: Provides agents with full context and tools to manage outbound calls efficiently while maintaining compliance.

screens.name: Campaigns List
screens.purpose: Overview of all outbound campaigns with status, agents, records, and coverage score. Entry point for campaign management.

components.label: Create campaign
components.action: Opens campaign creation wizard

components.label: Status badge
components.action: Shows Preparing / Scheduled / Running / Ready / Completed / Paused / Incomplete per campaign

components.label: Play/Pause/Stop controls
components.action: Start, pause, or stop a campaign inline

components.label: National DNC list scrubber
components.action: Toggle and manage DNC compliance

screens.name: Campaign General Settings (Step 1)
screens.purpose: Configure campaign name, description, timezone, date range, business hours, and assigned phone numbers including local presence.

components.label: Campaign name
components.action: Enter campaign title

components.label: Description (Optional)
components.action: Enter campaign description, 0/200 char limit

components.label: Timezone
components.action: Select campaign timezone (e.g. UTC-06:00 Central Time (US & Canada)), Profile time zone option available

components.label: Start date
components.action: Set campaign start date (optional), shows duration badge when dates set (e.g. '13 days')

components.label: End date
components.action: Set campaign end date (optional)

components.label: Business hours
components.action: Day-by-day schedule (Sunday-Saturday), each with start time, end time, AM/PM toggle, Full day checkbox. Monday-Friday checked by default.

components.label: Business Numbers
components.action: Select from list of numbers with + button to add multiple

components.label: Auto-rotation
components.action: Enable number rotation

components.label: Local Presence
components.action: Auto-select best number based on contact area code. Tooltip explains matching behavior.

components.label: Cancel
components.action: Discard campaign and return to list

components.label: Save
components.action: Save campaign progress

screens.name: Campaign Dialer Settings (Step 2)
screens.purpose: Select dialing mode (Preview, Progressive, Predictive, Agentless) and configure mode-specific parameters including timing, retry logic, and disposition options.

components.label: Mode description
components.text: Agent is provided with a preview of the contact's information before initiating the call

components.label: Preview time
components.action: Set time agent has to preview contact info

components.label: Wrap-up time
components.action: Post-call wrap time before next call

components.label: Max ring time
components.action: Maximum time to let contact phone ring

components.label: Default retry period
components.action: Minimum wait before retrying a record

components.label: Max att. per record
components.action: Maximum total dial attempts per contact

components.label: System disposition
components.action: Set Retry/Final rules with retry period and attempts per number for system-level outcomes

components.label: Agent disposition
components.action: Configure which outcome options agents can select, all marked as Final

components.label: Agent avatars
components.action: Add or remove agents from the campaign

screens.name: Campaign Record List (Step 4)
screens.purpose: Upload a new CSV contact list or select an existing record list. Shows list stats including list name, last changes (user + date), record count, and coverage score.

components.label: Create record list
components.action: Upload or drag and drop CSV file to create campaign record list

components.label: Download the template
components.action: Download CSV template for guidance

components.label: Available lists
components.action: Browse and select pre-uploaded lists showing record count and area codes

components.label: New record list
components.action: Create a new list rather than reusing existing

components.label: Coverage warning
components.text: Local presence may be less effective if some client area codes aren't covered by your selected numbers. Consider adding more matching ones.

components.label: Upload note
components.text: Files over 15 MB may take 5-7 minutes to upload

screens.name: Agent Workspace (Active Campaign)
screens.purpose: Agent interface during active preview dialer campaign showing contact info, conversation thread, and call outcome selection.

components.label: Wrap-up timer
components.action: Shows 'Wrap-up 00:30' countdown timer

components.label: Conversation thread (center)
components.action: Shows messages, images, call recordings with timestamps and duration

components.label: SELECT CALL OUTCOME (right)
components.action: Agent selects call disposition

components.label: Contact number display
components.action: Shows contact phone number (e.g. +1 904 515 4645)

components.label: Add the number to Do not call list
components.action: Adds contact number to Internal DNC list

user_flows.name: Create a Preview Dialer Campaign
user_flows.description: End-to-end flow for setting up a new outbound preview dialing campaign from scratch.

steps.action: Click 'Create campaign'
Enter campaign name and optional description (0/200 chars)
Select timezone (e.g. UTC-06:00 Central Time) and optionally set start/end dates
Configure business hours per day (Sunday-Saturday with AM/PM times or Full day)
Assign business numbers and optionally enable Auto-rotation and Local Presence
steps.result: Outbound caller IDs configured with local matching

steps.action: Select 'Preview' dialing mode tab
Configure timing: Preview time, Wrap-up time, Max ring time
steps.result: Call pacing configured for preview mode

steps.action: Set dialing attempts: Default retry period, Max attempts per record
Configure system dispositions (Retry/Final for busy, no response, abandoned, invalid, blocked)
Configure agent disposition options (Successful call, Call back later, etc.)
Add agents to campaign
Upload CSV or select existing record list
Review coverage score and address any coverage warnings
Save and launch campaign
steps.result: Campaign moves to Running or Scheduled status

user_flows.name: Agent Preview Call Flow
user_flows.description: Flow for an agent handling a call during an active preview dialer campaign.

steps.action: Agent receives preview of contact information
steps.result: Agent reviews contact details before calling

steps.action: Agent initiates the call when ready
Agent conducts conversation, views thread with messages/recordings
Call ends, wrap-up countdown begins (e.g. Wrap-up 00:30)
Agent selects call outcome from SELECT CALL OUTCOME panel
Optionally check 'Add the number to Do not call list'
steps.result: Contact added to Internal DNC list if checked

steps.action: Wrap-up timer expires, next contact preview presented

user_flows.name: Manage DNC Compliance
user_flows.description: Flow for enabling and managing Do Not Call list scrubbing for campaigns.

steps.action: Toggle on National DNC list scrubber
steps.result: System automatically removes registered numbers from campaign lists

steps.action: Manage Local DNC List via menu (Add more, Replace, Delete all, Export to CSV)
Agent checks 'Add the number to Do not call list' after a call
steps.result: Number added to Local DNC list in real-time

business_processes.name: Outbound Preview Dialing Campaign
business_processes.description: End-to-end business process for running a preview dialing outbound campaign where agents manually review contacts before calling.

steps.name: Create campaign with general settings (name, timezone, dates, hours, numbers)
Configure preview dialer mode and timing parameters
Configure system and agent dispositions
Assign agents to campaign
Upload contact record list (CSV) and review coverage score
Enable DNC list scrubber for compliance
Launch campaign
System presents contact preview to agent
Agent reviews contact information and decides to call
Agent conducts call
Agent selects disposition outcome
Agent optionally adds number to DNC list
System applies retry/final logic based on disposition
System retries or removes record from queue
Manager monitors campaign status and edits if needed

business_processes.name: DNC Compliance Management
business_processes.description: Process for ensuring outbound calls comply with Do Not Call regulations using both national and local DNC lists.
triggers: Agent marks contact as DNC during call

steps.name: Enable National DNC list scrubber toggle
System automatically scrubs campaign record lists against national DNC registry
Manage Local DNC List (Add more, Replace, Delete all, Export to CSV)
Agent adds contact number to DNC list via checkbox after call
System prevents future dialing of DNC-listed numbers

outcomes: Reduced TCPA and DNC violation risk

data_entities.name: Campaign
data_entities.description: An outbound calling campaign with configuration, staffing, scheduling and contact list.

fields.name: status
fields.description: Preparing / Scheduled / Running / Ready / Completed / Paused / Incomplete

fields.name: business_hours
fields.description: Per-day schedule (Sunday-Saturday) with start/end times, AM/PM, Full day option

fields.name: dnc_scrubber_enabled
fields.description: Whether National DNC list scrubber is active

data_entities.name: Dialer Settings (Preview Mode)
data_entities.description: Configuration for preview dialer mode within a campaign. Agent previews contact info before manually initiating the call.

fields.name: preview_time
fields.description: Time agent has to preview contact info before call

fields.name: max_ring_time
fields.description: Max time contact phone rings in seconds

fields.name: max_attempts_per_record
fields.description: Max total dial attempts per contact

data_entities.name: System Disposition
data_entities.description: Automated outcome handling rule for a specific system-level call result.

fields.name: outcome_type
fields.description: Contact busy / Contact no response / Agent abandoned / Invalid number / Blocked by spam filters / Blocked by robocall analytics

fields.name: retry_period
fields.description: Minutes before retry attempt (for Retry outcomes)

fields.name: attempts_per_num
fields.description: Max retries per number (for Retry outcomes)

data_entities.name: Agent Disposition
data_entities.description: Call outcome option that an agent selects after completing a call. In preview mode, all observed dispositions are marked as Final.

fields.name: is_final
fields.description: If true, contact is removed from dial queue

data_entities.name: Record List
data_entities.description: A CSV-based contact list uploaded for use in campaigns.

fields.name: area_codes
fields.description: Number of distinct area codes in the list

fields.name: coverage_score
fields.description: Fraction and percentage of area codes covered by local/overlay numbers

data_entities.name: Business Number
data_entities.description: A phone number assigned to a campaign for outbound caller ID, labeled by region.

fields.name: label
fields.description: Regional label (e.g. LA Main, LA Sales, NYC, Toronto, San Diego, Toll-Free)

data_entities.name: Local DNC List
data_entities.description: Internal Do Not Call list maintained within MightyCall. Numbers can be added by agents after calls or managed in bulk.

fields.name: numbers
fields.description: Phone numbers on the DNC list

ui_patterns.layout_type: Top navigation bar with dropdown sub-menus. 4-step wizard/stepper for campaign creation with left sidebar progress tracker. Two-column layout on creation forms (settings left, supporting panel right). Agent workspace uses three-panel layout (contacts left, conversation center, outcomes right).
ui_patterns.color_scheme: Dark navy/black top nav. White content area. Green accent for active nav items. Blue action buttons. Status badges with color coding per campaign status.
ui_patterns.component_library: Custom design system. Uses card panels, toggle switches, slider controls for timing settings, data tables with sortable columns, step-wizard sidebar, date pickers, AM/PM time inputs, drag-and-drop file upload zones, countdown timers.
common_patterns: 4-step campaign creation wizard with left sidebar progress tracker (General settings, Dialer settings, Agent list, Record list)
Tab-based dialer mode selector (Preview / Progressive / Predictive / Agentless)
Slider controls for timing parameters (Preview time, Wrap-up time, Max ring time)
Right-side available lists panel alongside main form in Record List step
Toggle switches for enable/disable features (Local Presence, Auto-rotation, DNC scrubber)
Inline status badges on campaign list screen
Disposition tables with Retry/Final selectors and retry period/attempts inputs
Coverage score metric linking local numbers to contact area codes with fraction and percentage display
Wrap-up countdown timer in agent workspace
SELECT CALL OUTCOME panel with radio options and DNC checkbox
Three-dot action menus for campaign and list management
Duration badge on date range (e.g. '13 days')
Play/pause/stop controls per campaign on list view
Local Presence tooltip with detailed explanation of matching behavior
